entries.headline: 2-3 on budget; totals went 1-3, NHL puck line saved the day — watchlist 8-6

picks.what_happened: Giants blew it open early — Casey Schmitt homered on the first pitch and Eric Haase hit a grand slam, putting SF up by nine runs. Crow was exposed far beyond his ERA; the Giants coasted to a 12-9 final.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — ERA trap fired on Crow exactly as modeled. Recent-form surge (5.1 R/G) validated: Giants scored 12. Houser's FIP accurately predicted his struggles.
picks.key_insight: ERA trap MILD (severity 0.26) still produced a dominant result — lower thresholds may still carry actionable edge.

picks.what_happened: VGK led 2-0 after two periods before Carolina exploded for three unanswered goals in 5:05 in the third. VGK tied it late with the goalie pulled, then Seth Jarvis scored a PP OT winner for CAR 4-3. Series tied 1-1.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Model projected an extremely tight game; final was decided by one goal in OT. The 2.9–2.9 projection captured the competitive parity perfectly.
picks.key_insight: NHL playoff model is performing well — the even projection + puck line approach is a high-confidence repeatable play in tight series.

picks.what_happened: Coby Mayo hit a bases-clearing double in a six-run Baltimore first inning. Baltimore's offense exploded before any of the model's structural advantages could materialize, handing Boston their worst loss of the series.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — All signals were structural advantages but a catastrophic first inning by Bello negated every positive RSox signal. The model had no first-inning explosion risk factor.
picks.key_insight: Brayan Bello's FIP 5.22 was the warning sign — the model may over-weight park/platoon when the starter profile itself is a liability.

picks.what_happened: Shea Langeliers went off with 2 HR and 3 RBI for the A's; Ian Happ and Pete Crow-Armstrong added HRs for Chicago. Four home runs inflated the total to 13 despite both starters pitching reasonably well into the middle innings.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — xFIP projections were accurate for starters but the model underestimated home-run variance at Wrigley. Park factor 1.03 was insufficiently weighted.
picks.key_insight: Wrigley HR rate may warrant a bespoke park adjustment for totals bets — xFIP alone doesn't capture wind/carry day-to-day variance at this park.

picks.what_happened: Both starters had trouble holding leads in a high-scoring 8-6 final. The Royals ran their offense to overcome a Minnesota lead. Twins' schedule load signal (7 games in 7 days) may have reflected fatigue in the bullpen late.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Projected 4-4, actual 8-6. The model severely under-estimated total run output. Morris's xFIP 4.00 vs FIP 3.03 was the warning — FIP was potentially luck-inflated.
picks.key_insight: When FIP–xFIP gap is large on both starters in the same game, the model should widen its projection range and reduce +1.5 line confidence.

picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — ERA trap SEVERE on Ginn correctly identified A's ML as overpriced; Cubs eked out a 1-run win.
INCORRECT — Chris Sale was projected as the dominant edge but Toronto's lineup lit up the Braves in the finale.
INCORRECT — ERA trap SEVERE on Wheeler predicted over-priced Phillies, but Wheeler delivered 7 strong innings. Padres' 2-8 form was the real liability.
CORRECT — ERA trap SEVERE on Wrobleski correctly flagged LAD ML as overpriced; ARI won outright.
INCORRECT — Model projected 8.4 total vs 9.0 line; actual was 14. Both starters surrendered far more than xFIP suggested.
CORRECT — ERA trap SEVERE on Teng correctly exposed Astros ML. Pirates' offense (6.8 R/G recent form) delivered emphatically.
CORRECT — Model projected 9.3 vs 8.5 line. Both teams' hot recent offense fully materialized in a high-scoring blowout.
CORRECT — Tight game as projected. Yankees won by 1; CLE +1.5 covered. Rodón ERA trap MILD appropriately muted the NYY edge.
CORRECT — TOR +1.5 covered easily as Blue Jays won outright 7-2.
INCORRECT — Dodgers' 8-2 recent form wasn't enough vs Ryne Nelson's solid outing.
CORRECT — Despite ERA trap SEVERE on Wheeler, he delivered 7 strong innings. Phillies' home advantage prevailed.
MIXED — Umpire Vic Carapazza (leans Over) was correctly identified; Over hit. Model's 7.9 projection was too low — Carapazza's impact was underweighted.
INCORRECT — Minnesota rookie Olivia Miles scored a career-high 28 pts with a WNBA-record 8 three-pointers. An outlier performance overrode the model's edge.
CORRECT — Kelsey Mitchell scored 25 pts as Fever won their Commissioner's Cup opener comfortably.

patterns: HIGH confidence budget: 2-2 (Giants ML ✓, VGK +1.5 ✓, Red Sox ML ✗, Under 10.5 ✗)
MLB totals: 1-3 today — Under 10.5 LOST (13 runs), Under 9.5 LOST (14 runs), Over 8.5 WON (16 runs), Under 8.5 LOST (10 runs)
Run line/puck line +1.5 spreads: 4-2 (VGK ✓, ARI ✓, Guardians ✓, BJ ✓ | Twins ✗, Padres ✗)
ERA trap picks (directional): 3-1 — Giants ML ✓, Cubs ML ✓, ARI +1.5 ✓ | Padres +1.5 ✗ (Wheeler defied his trap)
Recent-form surge signal: 2-1 (Giants 5.1 R/G validated; Pirates 6.8 R/G validated; RSox 5.4 R/G failed vs BAL first inning)
Umpire signals: 1-1 — Carapazza Over correctly identified (game went Over); Hudson large-K zone did not prevent Orioles explosion
NHL playoff model: 1-0 — Very sharp projection (2.9-2.9) yielded exact 1-goal margin
model_observations: Run scoring was dramatically underestimated in 3 of 5 MLB games (A's/Cubs actual 13 vs 8.8 proj; Royals/Twins actual 14 vs 8.4 proj; BAL/BOS actual 10 vs ~10 proj). The model may need a calibration pass on its June run environment assumptions.
ERA trap SEVERE (severity >1.0) correctly identified value in Cubs ML, ARI +1.5, and Pirates ML. Strong signal — consider raising budget allocation for picks driven by SEVERE traps.
ERA trap SEVERE on Wheeler (Phillies) was a notable failure: Wheeler pitched 7 strong innings. This suggests SEVERE traps on elite pitchers with small sample (<50 IP) deserve a discount.
xFIP as a total predictor underperformed today — 3 games dramatically exceeded xFIP-based projections. Investigate whether home run rate is a missing variable for June total models.
NHL puck line model is razor-sharp in the playoffs — the 2.9-2.9 projection yielding a 1-goal OT result justifies the +1.5 line approach in tight playoff series.
Schedule load warning appeared on 8 of today's budget/watchlist teams. Games involving teams on 7-in-7 (Twins) or 6-in-7 showed elevated offensive variance.

First-inning explosion risk is not modeled: Orioles scored 6 in the first; model had no mechanism to flag early-inning collapse probability for Bello (FIP 5.22 — the signal was there but not weighted).

entries.headline: 3-4 budget (+$5.58); Knicks & Mets delivered but parlays went 0-2, extras cost Rockies

picks.game: New York Knicks @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.why_picked: Blended NetRtg diff −8.5 in Knicks' favor, Spurs injury impact −1.9%, playoff intensity weighting 55% recent form
picks.what_happened: Jalen Brunson delivered a clutch late basket to lift the Knicks to a 105-104 nail-biter in Game 2 of the NBA Finals. Knicks lead series 2-0.
signals: Rating edge: NYK blended NetRtg diff −8.5
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — NetRtg edge and Spurs injury drag were decisive in a 1-point game; model tie projection (109-109) was directionally accurate
picks.key_insight: Knicks' superior net rating proved decisive even on Spurs home court; model's close-game call (109-109) confirmed edge is real but tight

picks.why_picked: Coors Field park factor 1.30, both pitchers FIP/xFIP regression candidates, Rockies recent form 4-6 with run uplift, model projected COL 6.0–MIL 5.7
picks.what_happened: Game was tied 5-5 through 9 after a wild ninth inning. In the 10th, Jake Bauers hit a tiebreaking two-run double for Milwaukee. Brewers pulled away 9-7.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Game was on-model through 9 innings (5-5 tie) but extra-inning mechanics voided the +1.5 spread; Rockies lost by 2 in extras
picks.key_insight: Coors extra-inning games amplify volatility; +1.5 run line picks here need an extras risk discount — Rockies effectively 'won' through regulation

picks.game: New York Mets @ San Diego Padres
picks.why_picked: ERA trap MODERATE on Michael King (ERA 3.18 vs xFIP 3.61, 68 IP); Padres 1-9 last 10, scoring only 2.6 R/G vs 3.9 season; Mets 5-5 recent but model projected 3.1-2.7
picks.what_happened: Christian Scott struck out batters and held San Diego scoreless over 5⅔ innings. Jared Young and Luis Torrens homered. Michael King (4-5) allowed 4 ER — his third straight loss.
signals: ERA trap MODERATE — Michael King ERA 3.18 vs xFIP 3.61
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — ERA trap fired perfectly; King's regression materialized in this very game. Padres' offensive slump validated the combined signal.
picks.key_insight: Pairing ERA trap with a team on a 1-9 cold streak is a high-confidence combination; this is a model archetype worth formalizing

picks.why_picked: ERA trap MODERATE on Anthony Kay (ERA 3.77 vs xFIP 4.28, severity 0.51); White Sox vs LHP platoon edge 0.771 OPS; ChiSox 7-3 last 10 running hot (5.8 R/G)
picks.what_happened: Phillies won 8-6 with Brandon Marsh homering off a LHP (first in ~4 years) and Kyle Schwarber collecting 4 singles. Jesús Luzardo (xFIP 2.60) was dominant, limiting White Sox impact.
signals: ERA trap MODERATE — Anthony Kay ERA 3.77 vs xFIP 4.28
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — ERA trap on Kay was offset by Luzardo's elite performance. Phillies offense also overcame their platoon disadvantage vs LHP.
picks.key_insight: When opposing starter xFIP < 3.00, an ERA trap on the other team is insufficient to create real edge — need an 'ace override' rule

picks.why_picked: ERA trap MODERATE on Robbie Ray; Giants recent form blazing hot (5.5 R/G vs 4.0 season, +0.46 adj); Cubs injury drag −1.2%; wind blowing OUT; model projected SF 4.7-4.6
picks.what_happened: Matt Chapman hit his 4th career grand slam + another HR for a career-high 8 RBIs. Giants hit 7 home runs total. Robbie Ray (4-6) threw 5 scoreless innings. Cubs dealt their 19th loss in 25 games.
signals: Giants recent form: 5.5 R/G vs 4.0 season (+0.46 adj)
ERA trap MODERATE — Robbie Ray ERA 4.45 vs xFIP 4.88
Wind 10 mph blowing OUT (SW) at Wrigley
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Giants' hot run-scoring form was the real engine; wind blowing out amplified the power explosion. Model's 51.2% win prob severely underestimated the gap.
picks.key_insight: When recent R/G uplift is +0.46 vs a team in 19-25 form, model probability should approach 60%+ not 51% — hot recent form is being discounted too heavily

picks.pick: Parlay: Colorado Rockies ML + New York Mets ML
picks.why_picked: Both legs had 12%+ edge; combining two HIGH-confidence picks for enhanced value ($6.31 profit on $1.69)
picks.what_happened: Mets leg hit easily (5-0 shutout). Rockies ML was sunk by extras — game was 5-5 through 9 but Milwaukee won in the 10th.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — One of two legs failed; Mets leg was spot-on but Rockies extras loss killed the ticket
picks.key_insight: Rockies ML and +1.5 both failed due to extras; parlaying a team in an extra-inning-risk game doubles the exposure — extras discount needed

picks.pick: Parlay: New York Knicks ML + Cleveland Guardians ML
picks.why_picked: Knicks HIGH confidence + Guardians MEDIUM (64.3%, edge 10.5%); cross-sport parlay for $6.42 profit on $1.58
picks.what_happened: Knicks leg hit (105-104 thriller). Guardians leg failed — Corey Seager returned from the IL and hit the go-ahead 2-run HR for Texas in their 3-2 win.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Guardians leg broke the parlay; Seager's IL return was a game-changing development not captured in the injury model
picks.key_insight: Verify Texas Rangers roster for IL returns before parlay inclusion; a key bat returning from IL is a direct signal invalidator

picks.why_picked: Yesavage FIP advantage, starter edge signals
picks.what_happened: Orioles cruised to a 9-5 win in Toronto.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Orioles won comfortably; starter edge held

picks.why_picked: Guardians model prob 64.3%, edge 10.5%; Parker Messick strong metrics (FIP 3.1 / xFIP 3.21)
picks.what_happened: Corey Seager returned from the injured list and hit a go-ahead 2-run HR off Messick. Rangers won 3-2.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Seager's IL return was a material roster update not reflected in the injury model; single swing decided the game
picks.key_insight: Model's injury data for Texas was stale — Seager returning is a critical edge-invalidator; pre-game lineup sync needed

picks.why_picked: Both starters FIP regression candidates; park factors moderate
picks.what_happened: 14 combined runs — well over the 7.5 line.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — High-scoring game confirmed both starters' xFIP regression

picks.why_picked: Twins +1.5; Matthews xFIP 3.22 strong; Wacha FIP 3.89 solid
picks.what_happened: Twins rallied to win 5-3; Brooks Lee's 6th-inning HR sparked a 4-run inning off Wacha. Byron Buxton left with injury.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Twins won outright; xFIP regression for Wacha materialized

picks.why_picked: Brady Singer xFIP 4.31 as regression candidate offset by Leahy; expected a moderate-scoring game
picks.what_happened: Cardinals blew out the Reds 10-3. Alec Burleson homered and scored 4 runs; Lars Nootbaar had multi-hit game in his season debut. 13 total runs.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Singer's massive FIP/xFIP gap (FIP 6.81) was a warning sign of collapse; Cardinals offense erupted
picks.key_insight: Singer's FIP 6.81 was a red flag for the Under; when a starter's FIP is this high, the Under side is risky regardless of xFIP

picks.why_picked: Both starters regression candidates, moderate combined FIP metrics suggested manageable scoring
picks.what_happened: Giants hit 7 home runs for 18 runs. Matt Chapman alone had 8 RBIs. 21 total runs — nearly double the line.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Wind signal (blowing OUT) and Robbie Ray's extreme FIP gap both pointed to potential offense explosion; Under was wrong side here
picks.key_insight: Wind blowing OUT at Wrigley + Ray's FIP 5.52 should have blocked the Under recommendation on this game

picks.why_picked: Braves starter Martín Pérez FIP 4.14 / xFIP 3.93; Pirates Keller FIP 3.35 (ERA regression candidate)
picks.what_happened: Mauricio Dubón hit a tying homer and then a go-ahead hit; Braves won 6-3.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Keller ERA trap fired; Braves won at home

picks.why_picked: Jack Perkins elite metrics (FIP 2.06 / xFIP 2.99); Astros +1.5 for safety margin
picks.what_happened: Isaac Paredes homered for the 3rd straight game with 4 RBIs; Astros won 5-1. Perkins was dominant.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Perkins' elite metrics held; Astros won outright

picks.why_picked: Ryan Gusto extreme ERA/xFIP gap (FIP 0.53 / xFIP 1.40) on Rays side; Marlins +1.5 as spread cover
picks.what_happened: Rays won 6-3. Marlins lost by 3 — +1.5 did not cover.
signals: Ryan Gusto FIP 0.53 / xFIP 1.40 / K9 12.0 (extreme positive for Rays)
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Gusto's FIP 0.53 (even with xFIP 1.40) was a dominant signal; Marlins +1.5 was fighting against an elite starter
picks.key_insight: Gusto's metrics were so dominant that Marlins +1.5 was questionable regardless of spread buffer; elite starter analysis must lead

picks.why_picked: Bryan Woo FIP 2.97 / xFIP 3.53 strong; Tigers +1.5 spread protection
picks.what_happened: Carpenter and Torkelson powered Detroit to a 7-3 win; Tigers are extending their winning streak.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Tigers won outright; Woo's strong metrics held

picks.game: Boston Red Sox @ New York Yankees
picks.why_picked: Ryan Weathers xFIP 2.97 elite; Sonny Gray FIP 3.44 / xFIP 3.54 strong; BOS +1.5 for spread safety
picks.what_happened: Red Sox won 5-3 outright at Yankee Stadium. Red Sox covered easily.
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Red Sox won outright; Weathers' strong xFIP (2.97) was a key positive signal

picks.game: Los Angeles Angels @ Los Angeles Dodgers
picks.why_picked: Roki Sasaki FIP 4.91 / xFIP 3.68 ERA trap candidate; Angels +1.5 spread protection
picks.what_happened: Dodgers shut out the Angels 6-0. Angels lost by 6; +1.5 didn't come close.
signals: Roki Sasaki FIP 4.91 / xFIP 3.68 (ERA trap signal vs Dodgers ML)
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Dodgers dominated; Angels were shut out. Spread buffer irrelevant when team is blanked
picks.key_insight: When a team gets shut out, the +1.5 is meaningless; Sasaki's ERA trap was not enough to lift Angels vs Dodger lineup depth

picks.why_picked: Wings on 4-game win streak; strong offensive metrics vs Sparks
picks.what_happened: Arike Ogunbowale led Dallas with 30 pts and 6 threes; Shepard added 22-15-5. Paige Bueckers had 14 assists for LA but it wasn't enough.
signals: Wings momentum: 4-game win streak, 6 of last 7
picks.signal_verdict: CORRECT — Wings covered comfortably; Ogunbowale was electric

picks.why_picked: Sun rated higher in offensive efficiency metrics
picks.what_happened: Chicago Sky won 85-80; Skylar Diggins scored 24 points and Elizabeth Williams hit two key baskets in the final minute.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Sky's home court and Diggins clutch play negated Sun's efficiency edge
picks.key_insight: WNBA home court advantage (and individual clutch performers) may need higher weight in late-game models

picks.why_picked: Portland home court; Fire 6-6 vs Mercury 4-8; value on home underdog
picks.what_happened: Mercury won 78-72 despite missing starters Thomas and Copper. DeWanna Bonner hit a clutch corner 3 for a 64-62 lead; Mercury never trailed again.
picks.signal_verdict: INCORRECT — Even without two starters Mercury prevailed; Bonner's veteran clutch performance was decisive
picks.key_insight: Mercury veteran resilience undermined the injury-load discount; depth matters more than expected when stars are out

patterns: HIGH confidence budget: 2-1 (Knicks WON, Mets WON; Rockies +1.5 LOST in extras)
MEDIUM confidence budget: 1-1 (Giants WON 18-3 blowout; White Sox LOST 6-8)
ERA trap signal: 1-1 (Mets/King fired CORRECT; failed to offset Luzardo's dominance INCORRECT)
Parlays: 0-2 (Rockies ML broken by extras; Guardians ML broken by Seager IL return)
Extra innings double-hurt: Rockies +1.5 AND Rockies ML parlay leg both LOST
Baltimore double hit: BAL ML + Over 7.5 both cashed (9-5, 14 combined runs)
MLB run-line underdogs: Twins +1.5, Astros +1.5, Tigers +1.5, BOS +1.5 all WON on watchlist
MLB totals under: 0-2 on watchlist (CIN U9.5 busted @13 runs; SF U11.5 busted @21 runs)
WNBA: 1-2 (Wings 104-96 WON; Sun upset 80-85 LOST; Portland Fire fell 72-78 LOST)
model_observations: Giants win probability only 51.2% but they won 18-3; recent run-scoring delta (+0.46 R/G adj) is severely underweighted when this large
Rockies +1.5 covered through 9 innings (5-5 tie) but extra innings added lethal volatility; Coors spread picks need extras discount
ERA trap (severity 0.43) on Michael King fired cleanly when paired with Padres 1-9 recent form — this combination is a reliable archetype
Luzardo (FIP 2.69 / xFIP 2.60 / K9 10.5) was too elite to fade via opponent ERA trap — add ace ceiling rule: suppress edge claims when opposing xFIP < 3.00
Guardians ML parlay leg failed because Corey Seager returned from IL and homered — pre-game IL/lineup scrape timing is critical for evening games
White Sox platoon edge (0.771 OPS vs LHP) did not materialize vs Luzardo K9 10.5 — extreme strikeout rates suppress platoon benefits; flag K9 > 10
NBA Finals model directionally accurate (predicted 109-109, actual 105-104 Knicks) — NetRtg edge of 8.5 was decisive in a 1-point game
WNBA Sun ML (HIGH confidence) lost 80-85 — verify WNBA injury/travel data freshness; Sky home court effect was underweighted
